pubmed_articles.title: X-ray Crystal Structures of the Influenza M2 Proton Channel Drug-Resistant V27A Mutant Bound to a Spiro-Adamantyl Amine Inhibitor Reveal the Mechanism of Adamantane Resistance.
Adamantane resistance in circulating human influenza A viruses from Alberta, Canada (1970-2007).

open_data_queries: ChEMBL target search: influenza A M2 proton channel

known_drug_rows.notes: Widespread resistance; CDC does not recommend adamantanes for currently circulating influenza A viruses

structure_rows.description: Influenza A M2 transmembrane peptide bound to amantadine

primary_structure.description: Influenza A M2 transmembrane peptide bound to amantadine

binding_site.description: Membrane channel binding site; not a straightforward soluble enzyme pocket.
key_residues: M2 channel pore residues; verify strain and resistance mutation state
binding_site.box_strategy: Requires membrane/channel-aware preparation; not recommended for first docking MVP.

computational_validation: Use only as historical control or method stress test.
Do not rank alongside NA without explicit caveats.
experimental_validation: Channel inhibition and resistance-specific viral assays would be needed.
closed_loop_assets.assay_path: Channel inhibition and resistance-specific viral assays would be needed.

evidence_quality.interpretation: Evidence score ranks source readiness for the computational closed loop; it is not a measured efficacy score.
